summary: Linked the common fighter item-pickup translation unit by making src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem.c build as Matching. The source change was small but targeted: ftpickupitem_800942A0 now gets fp->x294_itPickup through a new inline helper, which made that function report 100.00% matched. The PR also adjusted GALE01 symbol ownership for local strings, local float constants, and a local data label used by the linked object.

key_files.role: Added ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline and used it in ftpickupitem_800942A0 to close the remaining match gap.
Changed melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem.c from Object(NonMatching, ...) to Object(Matching, ...).
Updated local data/sdata2 symbol names and added ...data.0 so the newly linked object's symbols matched expected scope/naming.

what_changed: In configure.py, ftpickupitem.c was promoted from NonMatching to Matching.
In ftpickupitem.c, a new inline helper ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline(Fighter* fp) returns &fp->x294_itPickup.
ftpickupitem_800942A0 now assigns pickup via ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline(fp) instead of directly using &fp->x294_itPickup.
In symbols.txt, ftItemPickup_803C5580/assert_msg1/assert_msg2 were replaced by local compiler-style symbols @273/@274/@275, with ...data.0 added at 0x803C5580.
In symbols.txt, ftCo_804D8580, ftCo_804D8584, and ftCo_804D8588 were changed from global ftCo_* float symbols to local @253, @293, and @294 sdata2 symbols.
smart_moves: Used a minimal inline accessor around fp->x294_itPickup rather than rewriting the surrounding item-search logic, preserving readable semantics while changing codegen enough to match.
Linked the object only after updating both build classification and the symbol map, avoiding a partial source-only match that would fail at link/symbol ownership.
Demoted data symbols that behaved like local compiler-generated constants/strings instead of keeping misleading global ftCo_* or assert-style names.
decomp_lessons: For a nearly matching function, introducing a tiny inline helper around a struct-field address can affect generated code without changing behavior.
When promoting a TU to Matching, audit GALE01/symbols.txt for local-vs-global scope corrections; named globals may need to become @NNN locals.
A single function reaching 100% can be enough to justify flipping an entire object to Matching if the rest of the unit is already compatible.
Use the decomp.dev report as objective evidence: this PR reported 1 new match, ftpickupitem_800942A0 moving from 99.54% to 100.00%.
naming_conventions: Inline matching shim used the pattern <function_name>_inline: ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline.
Local data/string/float symbols in symbols.txt used compiler-style @NNN names such as @273, @274, @275, @253, @293, and @294.
The added local data label used the generated label spelling ...data.0 at the same address as @273.
Address-suffixed decomp names such as ftpickupitem_800942A0 and ftCo_804D8580 remained important search anchors, even when some symbols were demoted to locals.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Wrapped &fp->x294_itPickup in inline itPickup* ftpickupitem_800942A0_inline(Fighter* fp) to influence the emitted code for ftpickupitem_800942A0.
Kept the existing ground/air offset selection structure using fp->ground_or_air == GA_Ground ? &pickup->gr_light_offset : &pickup->air_light_offset.
Adjusted symbol scope for local strings and sdata2 float constants so linked data matched the object layout.
After source and symbol fixes, changed the build object entry to Object(Matching, "melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem.c").
review_feedback: No human PR body or review comments were present in the slice.
The decomp-dev bot reported GALE01 improvements: matched code 61.71% (+0.01%, +476 bytes), linked code 33.88% (+0.09%, +3332 bytes), and linked data 32.34% (+0.01%, +80 bytes).
The bot reported 1 new match: main/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftpickupitem item ftpickupitem_800942A0, +3 bytes, 99.54% to 100.00%.
